script_text: Longer fact texts have lots of little ideas.
But one big idea holds them all together.
That big idea is called the main topic.
Each paragraph focuses on a smaller part of the big idea.
Paragraph one might tell what something is.
Paragraph two might tell why it matters.
All paragraphs point back to the main topic.
Imagine a text about honey bees.
One paragraph on how they make honey. Another on where they live.
Main topic? Honey bees! Everything connects back to them.
After you read, ask: what is this text mostly about?
Now you are ready to find the big idea!
